last_result.message:
deterministic commands (never call the local model):
  search "query" | search query     - fill+submit the page search box
  open <link text>                  - navigate a same-origin link by visible text
  open!                             - confirm the last cross-origin open
  back                              - browser back
  scroll up | scroll down           - scroll the page
  extract links                     - list visible links (text + href)
  extract table                     - dump the first table as aligned text
  log                               - show this session's proposal/verdict audit log
  budget                            - show remaining LLM-call / executed-action rate-limit budget
  continue                          - resume after a rate-limit pause (M2.3)
  help                              - this text
  man <cmd>                         - detailed usage for one command (M3)
  clear                             - clear the output pane
  go <destination>                  - navigate anywhere — literal URL/domain, a defined alias, or (as a last resort) the local model resolves a destination from your typed words alone. First visit to a new origin (or any model-resolved destination) asks for confirmation.
  alias <name> = <command>          - define a single-command shortcut, e.g. alias wiki = go en.wikipedia.org (M3)
  unalias <name>                    - remove a defined alias (M3)
  macro <name> = <cmd1> && <cmd2>... - define a named && chain, depth-1 (a macro may not reference another macro) (M3)
  unmacro <name>                    - remove a defined macro (M3)
  origins                           - list origins visited by this tab this session (M3)
  dev on | dev off                  - toggle the data-lfl-state test hook (off by default — see docs/threat-model.md H2) (M3)

`cmd1 && cmd2 && ...` chains up to 5 ordinary commands (M3) — quote-aware,
any error/block/rejection/Esc clears the rest of the chain.

anything else, or "ask <...>", is sent to the local model as ONE proposed
action. click/fill/select/navigate require your approval (Enter/click
Approve, or Esc/click Reject).

command: ask take me to the CSS documentation

proposal.reason: CSS documentation link is directly available in the element list

command: ask search for javascript array documentation

expect.note: known 4B failure mode: clicking a search button instead of filling the searchbox — the few-shot in service-worker.js specifically targets this. Prefixed 'ask ' (security review SHOULD-FIX #8): the un-prefixed form 'search for ...' matches engine.js's deterministic /^search\s+(.+)$/i regex and never reaches the model at all, so this case was never actually exercised before the fix.

proposal.reason: clicking the JavaScript link leads to documentation

last_result.message: This page is about MDN Web Docs, a resource for learning and referencing web development technologies such as HTML, CSS, JavaScript, and Web APIs.

command: ask open the JavaScript reference page

expect.note: prefixed 'ask ' (security review SHOULD-FIX #8): the un-prefixed form 'open ...' matches engine.js's deterministic /^open\s+(.+)$/i regex and never reaches the model at all.
